evidence: The amber probe plus four periwinkle candidates and their lilac score bars form one coherent system with the probe as the moving focus; nothing else competes.
The frame presents the probe, four candidates, and four score bars as a single covarying interactive rather than four unrelated novel ideas.
Labels c1..c4 and 'probe' sit at their arrow tips and each score bar carries its own c1..c4 caption; no legend or key is present.
On-screen text is limited to the instructional annotation and object labels, all referring to visible arrows and bars.
The title, candidate labels, and mono score digits are all rendered legibly at the shown resolutions including the 375px frame.
The unit circle, all arrows, labels, and the row of score bars sit fully inside every frame with margins.
In orthogonal-1280 the amber 'probe' label is stamped over the 'c3' label, and in initial-1280 and initial-375 the 'probe' label overlaps the 'c1' label and dot.
suggestion: Offset the probe's label dynamically so it clears candidate tip labels as the probe sweeps past c1 and c3.

evidence: Amber probe, periwinkle candidates, lilac score bars, and cream labels are used consistently across all states with no concept switching color.
Every element carries content; the amber circle reads as the unit-length reference for the vectors, not decoration.

Each driven state matches its promise: aligned peaks c1 at 1.0, orthogonal peaks c3 with c1 at 0.0, anti-aligned drops c1 to -1.0 below baseline, and length-cheat stretches c1 to a bolded 1.3 past c2's 1.0.
The score bars sit below the vectors but each is captioned with its candidate name, keeping the arrow-to-bar link readable.
not applicable: frames are independent driven states rather than a continuous build sequence.
The composition has generous margins and clear negative space between the vector cluster and the score row.
